message:
fix(status/health/diag/doctor/search/asset-state): stop creating an empty index dir when probing a fresh install

`status`, `health`, `diag`, `doctor`, `search`, and the asset-state
inspector all need to know where the lexical index lives on disk. They
were each calling `crate::search::tantivy::index_dir(data_dir)`, which
— by virtue of going through the `fs_index_dir` FFI — has the side
effect of creating the directory tree if it does not exist.

That's fine when the caller is about to open or write the index, but
all six of these call sites only *inspect* the path. On a fresh install
(no `agent_search.db`, no `index/` tree) every single one of these
commands was silently creating `data_dir/index/v4/` with nothing in it,
and then immediately reporting `healthy: false, initialized: false`.
Nice and consistent, but it left a useless empty directory behind on
every run, and — worse — produced tests whose "fresh install" fixture
was polluted the moment the assertion ran.

Fix: add a new `expected_index_dir(base: &Path) -> PathBuf` helper in
`src/search/tantivy.rs` that composes the path from `CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION`
directly (the same logic the FFI uses internally) without creating it.
All read-only inspection call sites switch to the new helper:

  * `cass_lexical_index_initialized(data_dir)` (src/lib.rs)
  * `state_meta_json(...)` (src/lib.rs)
  * `run_cli_search(...)` (src/lib.rs)
  * `run_diag(...)` (src/lib.rs)
  * `run_doctor(...)` (src/lib.rs)
  * `inspect_lexical_assets(...)` (src/search/asset_state.rs)

`index_dir()` itself is preserved unchanged for call sites that
actually intend to open or write the index (the indexer itself, the
rebuild path, the commit-interval helpers). The two functions now have
a clear division: `expected_index_dir` is a pure path computation,
`index_dir` is side-effecting on disk.

Tests
-----

`tests/cli_robot.rs` gets two new assertions on the fresh-install
fixtures:

  * `status_missing_db_reports_not_initialized`: after running
    `cass status`, `tmp/index` must not exist. Pre-fix, it did.
  * `health_missing_db_reports_not_initialized`: after running
    `cass health`, `tmp/index` must not exist. Pre-fix, it did.

Both assertions would have failed against the old code. Also asserts
the JSON response includes `state.index.exists == false` / `index.exists
== false` so the no-side-effect path is visible to callers.

Tantivy re-export list updated to expose `CASS_SCHEMA_VERSION` in
addition to the hash constant, so the new helper can compose the path
without guessing.

No behavior change for callers that already had a populated index
tree; this only changes the first-run / fresh-install experience.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
